science.title: Scientists just created exotic new forms of matter that shouldn’t exist
science.summary: A new quantum physics study reveals that simply changing a magnetic field over time can unlock entirely new forms of matter that don’t exist under normal conditions. By carefully “driving” materials with timed magnetic shifts, researchers created exotic quantum states that could be far more stable and resistant to erro

science.title: MIT scientists finally reveal the hidden structure of a mysterious high-tech material
science.summary: For decades, relaxor ferroelectrics have powered everything from medical ultrasounds to sonar systems, yet their inner atomic structure remained a mystery—until now. Researchers have finally mapped their three-dimensional structure in unprecedented detail, uncovering hidden patterns in how electric charges are arranged

science.title: Astronomers finally solve the gamma-Cas X-ray mystery after 50 years
science.summary: A decades-old cosmic mystery has finally been cracked: the strange X-rays coming from the bright star gamma-Cas are caused by a hidden stellar companion feeding off it. science.title: Quantum Breakthrough: New Algorithm Solves “Impossible” Materials in Seconds
science.summary: A new quantum-inspired algorithm is reshaping how scientists approach some of the most complex materials known, enabling rapid analysis of structures that were previously beyond computational reach. Quantum technologies, including quantum computers, rely on materials that display unusual quantum effects under specific

science.title: Scientists turn plastic waste into clean hydrogen fuel using sunlight
science.summary: Scientists are using sunlight to turn plastic waste into clean fuels like hydrogen, offering a breakthrough solution to both pollution and energy challenges. While still in development, the approach could transform trash into a valuable resource for a low-carbon future.

tech.title: Welcome to the Great American Satellite Age
tech.summary: A new generation of satellite startups in San Francisco is racing to capitalize on recent technological breakthroughs in space-based data collection and communications.

tech.title: Mac mini starting price goes up to $799, may be hard to get for "months"
tech.summary: Chip shortages and demand from AI enthusiasts are both playing a part.

tech.title: PayPal says it’s ‘becoming a technology company again’ — that means AI
tech.summary: PayPal is pitching an AI-led turnaround, tying automation and restructuring to $1.5 billion in savings as it cuts jobs and works to modernize its tech stack.

tech.title: Kaspersky suspects Chinese hackers planted a backdoor into Daemon Tools in ‘widespread’ attack
tech.summary: The cybersecurity company says it's seen thousands of infection attempts, and at least a dozen successful hacks after users installed malicious versions of the popular Windows software.

tech.title: Pornhub Restores Access for UK Adults Who Use Apple’s Age Verification
tech.summary: The porn giant blocked its content from new UK users in February but says device-based age verification is more secure than third-party sites.

medicine.title: New treatment cuts bad cholesterol by nearly 50% without statins
medicine.summary: A new breakthrough could change how high cholesterol is treated, offering a powerful alternative to traditional drugs. Researchers have developed tiny DNA-based molecules that shut down PCSK9—a key protein that keeps “bad” LDL cholesterol circulating in the blood. medicine.title: Brain-based index may reveal Alzheimer's risk patterns in adults as young as 30

medicine.title: Breakthrough biomaterial heals tissue from the inside out
medicine.summary: Scientists have developed a breakthrough injectable biomaterial that travels through the bloodstream to repair damaged tissue from within, reducing inflammation and jumpstarting healing. In animal studies, it successfully treated heart attack damage and even showed promise for conditions like traumatic brain injury and

medicine.title: A forgotten drug is giving new hope to kids with a rare disease
medicine.summary: A decades-old drug once used to treat sleeping sickness is now showing surprising promise against an ultra-rare and life-threatening genetic disorder called Bachmann-Bupp syndrome (BABS). Early patient treatments suggest the drug, DFMO, may ease severe symptoms by targeting the underlying genetic malfunction. Researche

medicine.title: Simple “gut reset” may stop weight gain after Ozempic or Wegovy
medicine.summary: A new minimally invasive procedure may help people keep weight off after stopping popular drugs like Ozempic and semaglutide—something most patients struggle with. mathematics.title: In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far?
mathematics.summary: The quest to make mathematics rigorous has a long and spotty history — one mathematicians can learn from as they push to formalize everything in the computer program Lean. mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: School of Science welcomed new faculty in 2024
mathematics.summary: Eleven new professors join the departments of Biology; Brain and Cognitive Sciences; Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences; Mathematics; and Physics.

mathematics.title: Laurent Demanet appointed co-director of MIT Center for Computational Science and Engineering
mathematics.summary: Applied mathematics professor will join fellow co-director Nicolas Hadjiconstantinou in leading the cross-cutting center.

mathematics.title: What Can We Gain by Losing Infinity?

space.title: NASA’s Perseverance Mars Rover Surveys ‘Crocodile Bridge’
space.summary: Description NASA’s Perseverance Mars rover used its Mastcam-Z camera system to capture this 360-degree panorama of a region nicknamed “Crocodile Bridge” on Jezero Crater’s rim. astronomy.title: The Paranal solar ESPRESSO Telescope, a New Tool for Finding Exoplanets
astronomy.summary: The Paranal solar ESPRESSO Telescope (PoET), installed at the European Southern Observatory's (ESO's) Paranal site in Chile, has made its first observations. astronomy.title: What is the Most Common Type of Planet in the Galaxy?
astronomy.summary: Astronomers now believe there is at least one planet for every star in the Milky Way but new research has revealed a deeply unsettling twist in that picture. astronomy.title: Binary Stars Form Lots Of Exoplanets, But Many Of Them Are Ejected As Rogue Planets
astronomy.summary: Binary stars are common, but for a long time astronomers have thought that exoplanets would have trouble forming around them. astronomy.title: Lasers take aim at a galaxy far, far away | Space photo of the day for May 4, 2026
astronomy.summary: Looking like a scene out of "Star Wars," this image shows the ESO's Very Large Telescope Interferometer (VLTI) beaming four separate lasers into the sky towards the Tarantula Nebula.

astronomy.title: Meet the Constellations: Leo, the Lion
astronomy.summary: Leo, the Lion, is one of the most recognizable of the spring constellations, with its large size, distinctive shape, and plentiful bright stars. The post Meet the Constellations: Leo, the Lion appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

architecture.summary: The Museum of Furniture Studies was founded in 2017 in Stockholm, showcasing a collection of more than 1,300 furniture pieces by over 44 international designers. architecture.title: Rockpool House / Architecture Saville Isaacs

architecture.title: Villa 68 / MAS Architecture Workshop
architecture.summary: The Villa 68 is located in a new urban area in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. architecture.title: SOM "preserves the soul" of 1960s modernist office complex in Milan
architecture.summary: Global studio SOM has renewed Corso Italia 23, a 1960s office complex in Milan, retrofitting its modernist structure and fractured interiors to align with modern standards.
